title: Rate limiting: cap broadcast/send at 120 msg/min per user
description:
Implement per-session message rate limiting as specified in IMPROVEMENTS.md §2.

What: Add DashMap-based rate limiter to AppState; return JSON-RPC error -32020 RATE_LIMITED when exceeded.

Why: Currently a subscribed client can broadcast thousands of messages per second. Rate limiting prevents abuse without requiring payment per message.

Algorithm:
1. Add dashmap = '6' dep (already in workspace).
2. Add RateLimitState struct to relay/src/state.rs: DashMap<String, (u32, Instant)> keyed by pub_id.
3. Extend AppState::Inner with rate_limit field.
4. In ws.rs broadcast/sealed_broadcast/group_send handlers: call check_rate_limit(pub_id, now) before processing.
5. check_rate_limit: if entry missing or Instant older than 60s, reset to (1, now). Else if count >= 120, return Err(RATE_LIMITED). Else increment.
6. Return JSON-RPC error: code=-32020, message='rate limited'.

Apply to: broadcast, sealed_broadcast, group_send. Not whisper (1:1 has natural cost).

Defensive programming:
- Rate limiter never panics on lock failure (DashMap is lock-free).
- Clock reads use std::time::Instant (monotonic).
- Counter wrap not possible: u32::MAX >> 120.

Tests:
- test_rate_limit_allows_120_per_window: send 120 broadcast calls, verify all succeed.
- test_rate_limit_blocks_121st: send 121 calls, verify 121st returns -32020.
- test_rate_limit_resets_after_window: send 120, advance time by 61s, send 1 more, verify success.
close_reason: rate limiting already implemented in relay/src/ws.rs; IMPROVEMENTS.md item pre-empted
